summary_one_line: Token must begin with a hex-integer literal: '0' followed by 'x'/'X' then a hex digit (the SQL 0x prefix) to take the true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A token whose first bytes form the SQL hex-integer prefix: z[0]=='0' AND (z[1]=='x' OR z[1]=='X') AND z[2] is a hex digit, i.e. the literal '0x' (or '0X') immediately followed by [0-9a-fA-F].
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every value_profile_cmplog winning seed contains an explicit '0x' hex prefix (e.g. 'SELECT-0x85E', 'SELECT -0x11CCC1', 'SELECT -0x111111', 'SELECT -0xC11180'), so z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x', z[2] hex digit all hold and the true (hex-integer) side of line 159595 is taken.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds carry decimal digit runs without an 'x'/'X' after a '0' (e.g. '00S', 'SELECT.5-5/55555', 'SELECT.5E1-55'); z[1] is never 'x'/'X', so the (z[1]=='x'||z[1]=='X') sub-condition at 159595:25 evaluates false and the true side is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers carry the same input-to-state substitution mutator; the decisive delta is value_profile. value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward partial Hamming/prefix progress on the chained byte comparisons (z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x'/'X', isxdigit(z[2])), guiding the corpus to assemble the multi-byte '0x' prefix that pure cmplog (edge-bucket-only feedback) cannot reward incrementally.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker at line 159595 is the SQL hex-integer prefix test z[0]=='0' && (z[1]=='x'||z[1]=='X') && sqlite3Isxdigit(z[2]); its true side is globally blocked.
The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog > cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile.
The winning value_profile_cmplog seeds contain an explicit hex prefix '0x' (the feature byte pattern).
Another winning seed also carries the '0x' hex prefix, confirming the feature is consistent across winners.
The loser-blocking cmplog seeds contain decimal/non-hex bytes (digit runs, no 'x'/'X' after a '0'), so they cannot satisfy the prefix test.
At the blocker, the winner takes the true side while the loser does not (W T=10 F=4 vs L T=0 F=26 at 159595:25).
value_profile_cmplog's feedback includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets in addition to edge buckets, the technique cmplog lacks.
Plain cmplog's feedback is only the same edge-bucket signal as naive; the CMP buffer is consumed by the mutator, not feedback, so it cannot reward partial byte-match progress.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

evidence_trail.claim: Both involved fuzzers carry I2SRandReplace, so I2S cannot be the differentiator; value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient is the added technique in the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage on winning vpc seed 06e09a37e739e806 (trial 2) showed I2S-floor dash rows at depth 9 and 10, but I2S is present in BOTH involved fuzzers so it is not the decisive delta. The decisive delta is value_profile, whose CMP_MAP gradient feedback (absent from plain cmplog, whose feedback is edge-buckets only) rewards partial progress toward the chained 0x-prefix byte comparisons; CMP_MAP gradient is a feedback mechanism not recorded as a lineage mutator row, so it is not directly verifiable via db_query.py lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the same 0x-hex-prefix comparison in which plain cmplog resolves the true side as fast as value_profile_cmplog (no value_profile advantage), showing the CMP_MAP gradient is not what enables the winner.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-set overlap at offsets 0x06-0x09 between W and L is partial, so attributing the win specifically to forming the 'x'/'X' byte rather than to broader corpus quality from value_profile rests on the seed examples rather than an isolated single-byte diff.
